wo.def: An executable order to build a specific quantity of a product. It consumes materials and reports production on the floor.

plan.def: A planning lane that schedules and sequences future work. It groups and orders Work Orders but is not executed directly on the floor.

bom.def: The structured list of components and quantities required to build a product.

routing.def: The ordered sequence of operations and workcenters a product passes through to be built.

kitting.def: Gathering all the materials for a Work Order into a kit before production starts.

backflush.def: Automatically deducting consumed materials from inventory based on what was produced, rather than scanning each part.

ctb.def: A readiness check confirming that all materials needed for a Work Order are available so it can start.

mes.def: The shop-floor system operators use to execute Work Orders, report production and consume materials in real time.

npi.def: The process of taking a new product from design into stable, repeatable manufacturing.

wip.def: Products that have started but not yet finished production.

wms.def: The system that manages inbound, storage, picking and outbound of materials in the warehouse.

mrp.def: Planning logic that calculates what materials to buy or build, and when, to meet demand.

andon.def: A signaling system that lets operators flag problems on the line so support can respond quickly.

genealogy.def: The full as-built traceability of a unit: which materials, lots and operations went into it.
